metadata.summary: Python Bindings for Cisco ACI REST API

documentation:
# PyACI: Python Bindings for Cisco ACI REST API

```{image} https://codecov.io/gh/datacenter/pyaci/branch/master/graph/badge.svg
:alt: codecov status
:target: https://codecov.io/gh/datacenter/pyaci
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/pyaci/badge/?version=latest&style=plastic
:alt: Doc Status
:target: http://pyaci.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
```

## Documentation

Documentation is available at <http://pyaci.readthedocs.io/en/latest/>

## Examples

Jupyter Notebook based examples can be found in [examples](examples) directory.

modules.name: pyaci
documentation:
PyACI - Python Bindings for ACI REST API
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

PyACI is a simplified take on providing Python bindings for Cisco ACI
REST API.

modules.name: pyaci.utils
documentation:
pyaci.utils
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

This module contains PyACI utility functions.
Copyright (c) 2014 Cisco Systems, Inc. All rights reserved.

functions.name: pyaci.utils.getParentDn
documentation: Get the parent DN of the given DN string.

functions.name: pyaci.utils.splitIntoRns
documentation: Split the given DN string to a list of RN strings.

functions.name: pyaci.utils.readOnlyTree
documentation:
Make the given MO tree read-only.

The MO tree (starting from topRoot) remains read-only until the
enclosing with statement returns.

functions.name: pyaci.utils.digestConfigExport
documentation:
Digest an APIC config export file into the given topRoot.

```
param path

path to the config export file.

param topRoot

topRoot MO.
```

functions.name: pyaci.utils.distributeConfig
documentation:
Distribute configuration from the given root MO into multiple
roots.

In order for an MO tree to be posted to APIC in a single
operation, all MOs in that tree should belong to the same
shard. APIC will reject if any of the MOs is not in the same
shard. This function splits the given tree to multiple subtrees,
ensuring that all the MOs in the subtre belong to the same context
root. One can later post the individual subtrees without worrying
about the context root implications.

```
param root

MO at the top of the tree to be processed.

param result

optional list of subtrees to append the result to.
```

functions.name: pyaci.utils.mergeRoot
documentation: Merge the given root MO into the given acculumulator.

modules.name: pyaci.templating
documentation:
pyaci.templating
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

This module contains helpers to use Jinja2 templates with PyACI.
Copyright (c) 2014, 2015 Cisco Systems, Inc. All rights reserved.

functions.name: pyaci.templating.mergeDict
documentation:
Merge the given two dictionaries recursively and return the
result.

classes.name: pyaci.templating.TemplateRepository

methods.name: pyaci.templating.TemplateRepository.render
documentation:
Render a given template.

Template values can be specified using a YAML file, or a
Python dictionary, or both. When both are specifed, the values
are merged, and the value specified in the dictionary takes
precedence if there is a conflict.

```
param template_path

path to the template within the repository.

param template_values_file

path to the template values YAML file.

param template_values

template values dictinary.
```

A Jinja2 template repository.

```
param path

path to a directory containing Jinja2 templates.
```

Usage:

```
>>> import pyaci
>>> repo = pyaci.TemplateRepository('path/to/repo')
>>> xml1 = repo.render('template1.xml', template_values={'count': 2})
>>> xml2 = repo.render('template2.xml',
                        template_values_file='values2.yml')
>>> json1 = repo.render('template1.json',
                         template_values_file='values1.yml',
                         template_values={'name': 'bar'})
```

modules.name: pyaci.core

pyaci.core

This module contains the core classes of PyACI.

variables.name: pyaci.core.DELTA
documentation: time delta to allow for any variations of clock...

methods.name: pyaci.core.Backup.__init__
documentation: This class uses a few of the previously defined classes and methods but  is not a subclass of any of them.
As such I am using composition to include the methods and classes that I need to use.

class_variables.name: pyaci.core.AutoRefreshThread.REFRESH_BEFORE
documentation: approx - this many seconds before expiry, do token refresh

class_variables.name: pyaci.core.AutoRefreshThread.CHECK_INTERVAL
documentation: how long to sleep before waking to check for any work to do

class_variables.name: pyaci.core.AutoRefreshThread.WS_REFRESH_INT
documentation: approx - this many seconds before subscription refresh

methods.name: pyaci.core.RefreshSubscriptionsMethod.__call__
